description: Asr-e Azadegan (عصر آزادگان, 'Age of the Free People') was the fourth and final iteration of [Mashallah Shamsolvaezin](/people/mashallah-shamsolvaezin)'s serial newspaper chain, assembling staff from all three predecessors. [Akbar Ganji](/people/akbar-ganji) wrote landmark investigative columns on the chain murders of 1998 in its pages. Closed in the April 2000 crackdown. It was succeeded by the weekly *Gunagoun* under editor Fatemeh Farahmandpour — which the Press Court explicitly closed for 'closely resembling Jame'eh, Tous, Neshat, and Asr-e Azadegan.'
